title: sideritis aculeata
intro: Sideritis aculeata, also known as Greek mountain tea or prickly ironwort, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Sideritis aculeata include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce anxiety. Herbal preparations of Sideritis aculeata can be made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed as a natural remedy for various health issues, or used topically for skin and wound care.
